rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMUNDO LUNA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/19/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No additional bond has been filed. 3) Please provide a proof of recording of the transfer of 50% ownership of the 121 Lisbon Street property to the trust. 4) The security deposits received and refunded during the account period must be allocated to principal. See Probate Code section 16356. 5) The security deposits remaining at the end of the account period must also be listed on the schedule of liabilities. 6) The legal fees for eviction must be allocated one-half to principal and one-half to income. See sections 16370(b) and 16371(a)(1). 7) Please explain why the bond premiums should not be allocated to income. See section 16370(c) & (d). 8) Schedule C-1b reports repayment of $114,568.66 to Andres Luna from the beneficiary's personal account, but according to the petitioner's declaration filed on 05/19/2022, the repayment was made from the trust. 9) Should the correct total costs be $1,729.24 + $215.21 + $460.33 = $2,404.78? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order For Approval Of Resignation Of Wengai Zhao; Authority Of Joint Guardian To Act; And For Issuance Of Amended Letters Of Guardianship Of The Estate To Gene Yue
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/26/2023 to be calendared with the related status hearings. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No GC-020 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) The resigning co-conservator of the estate must join in the petition. See Probate Code section 2660. 3) Direct notice must be given to the guardian of the person, absent a waiver of such notice signed by the guardian of the person or her attorney of record. Note: As no order waiving accounts has been issued, the resigning co-guardian's final account will be required before the Court can discharge the resigning co-guardian and authorize the clerk to issue amended letters to the remaining co-guardian.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES HAROLD ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the amended petition was verified on 5/24/22 by the administrator with will annexed (not the "administrator" nor the "executor"), there was no Exhibit B attached to the amended petition, as alleged in paragraph 17 of the amended petition, there was no bond required in this proceeding, as alleged in paragraph 9, there was no real property on hand for distribution because there was never real property in the estate, the creditor claims period expired on 9/14/19, not 9/26/19, an accounting for a decedent's estate should always begin with the date of death, and it appears that the administrator with will annexed paid a third party $487.40 to prepare the accounting, which is part of the personal representative's ordinary duties and should reduce the statutory compensation.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES KAVANAGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/27/2023. 1) The LLC's income and expenses are not part of the estate's accounting receipts and disbursements, unless the estate actually received distributions from the LLC or paid the LLC's expenses from the estate's bank accounts. 2) The appraised value of the estate's interest in the LLC is $13,513,500 (Attachment 2, Item 3, of the inventory and appraisal). If the referee did not take into account the LLC's bank account (First Republic Bank #1579) in fixing the value, the LLC interest must be reappraised as a single item. The LLC bank account (Attachment 1, item 2, of the inventory and appraisal) must be removed from cash assets. 3) The Banner Bank account appears to be a joint account. Please explain why the account should not belong to the two surviving joint holders by operation of law (it should be noted that the decedent's name was removed from the account title as of February 2022). 4) The First Republic Bank account #8945 appears to be a joint account. Please explain why the account should not belong to the petitioner as the surviving joint holder by operation of law (it should be noted that this account was not identified in the Memorandum of Agreement between the petitioner and decedent entered into in connection with their divorce proceeding). 5) Is the estate's 34.5535% interest in the San Jacinto Way property the decedent's separate property, as the property was divided by the Memorandum of Agreement? 6) A legal description of the Wood Street property must be provided in the petition and proposed order. 7) The proof of service of notice did not list the attorney for Aidan who previously made an appearance at the hearing and has not been relieved as counsel. 8) No timely election was filed by the petitioner as the surviving spouse under Probate Code section 13502 to probate her intestate shares of the estate otherwise passing to her without administration. No court approval has been sought for a late election. 9) No attachments or exhibits can be used for the proposed order. Each distribution provision must be set forth in the body of the order. A specific amount of net cash distributable to each beneficiary, after payment of fees and costs sought to be approved, must be stated. Non-cash assets must be distributed in kind, absent a non-pro rata allocation agreement signed by all the beneficiaries. 10) Is the Memorandum of Agreement a complete property settlement operating as the petitioner's waiver of her right to receive any part of the estate by intestate succession under sections 141(a)(1) & 145? See also Welch v. Welch (2022) 79 Cal.App.5th 283. Note: Fees and costs of the guardian ad litem and his attorney must be determined before the Court can order final distribution. The guardian ad litem must join in the petition or file a separate petition to request his and his attorney's fees and costs.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES EDWARD DOHERTY JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or BENEFICIARY has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (2) The calculation of the statutory compensation appears to be incorrect. Did the executor and the attorney intend to request lower compensation? (3) Exhibit D reflects e-filing costs on 1/5/22 and 3/13/22 for an order; proposed orders should NOT be e-filed and must be provided in hard copy. [LRSF 14.59 E.] (4) The cost to lodge a will is ordinarily $50, not $94, and the initial petition and this petition were each $435, not $445 or $525. (5) If some of the tangible personal property was already distributed to the Salvation Army (under what authority?), why is the full value of the tangible personal property ($4,300) still on hand? (6) Because the jewelry, specific items of furniture, and artwork, etc., were not separately appraised, are the beneficiaries of those items in agreement with the proposed distribution without valuation? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/18/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no need to give notice to parties who have already been determined not to be interested parties. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENDY FU TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 28, 2003, AND AMENDMENTS THERETO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Amended Petition By Trust Beneficiary Valen Wong To Redress Breach Of The Wendy Fu Trust By Respondent Wanda Lee, Trustee And For Accounting Pursuant To C.C.P. 435 And 436; And To Dismiss Pursuant To C.C.P 581(F)(3); Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Tentative Ruling by Judge Stephen M. Murphy Respondent/Trustee Wanda Lee's Motion to Strike Amended Petition and to Dismiss Pursuant to CCP section 581(f)(2) is DENIED. The Court exercises its discretion and accepts the late filing of the Amended Petition. Respondent has twenty (20) days to respond to the Amended Pleading. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HERCULES MOSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Status Report And Petition For Termination Of Trust And Discharge Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 05/10/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) Re the DE-120 form filed on 05/24/2023: i) The CourtCall information listed on the form is incorrect. ii) Item 1 identified the guardian ad litem as the petitioner, but this petition was filed by the trustee. iii) Notice must be given to the Departments of Developmental Services and State Hospitals at their current addresses (see DE-120 form filed on 03/24/2023). New notice must be given. iv) Notice must be given to the guardian ad litem who is not joining in this petition. 2) Did the guardian ad litem and petitioner execute the joinder agreement prior to the beneficiary's death? If not, has the beneficiary's sub-account with the master pooled trust ever been established so as to require termination thereof? 3) Has the $103,000 bond ever been issued to the petitioner so as to require exoneration thereof? 4) It is unclear whether the petition intended to request termination of the master pooled trust in its entirety or the deceased beneficiary's sub-acccount without affecting the master pooled trust. If the master pooled trust is sought to be terminated, all other beneficiaries whose sub-accounts have been established must be identified and receive notice. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GREGSON 1996 TRUST, DATED JUNE 28, 1996
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of John M Gregson For Court Construction Of Trust Order Instructing Transfer Of Interest In Real Property Surcharge Damages For Beneficiary Anne Marie O Hallorans Bad Faith Challenge To Trustee Actions Attorneys? Fees And Costs Probate Code 850 17200
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues include: i. Notice of Hearing on the proper Judicial Council Form has not been given (DE-115) as to the request made under Probate Code 850. ii. Direct notice was not served on Anne-Marie O'Halloran (direct notice is required in addition to notice on the attorney). Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE DOWLER DICKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/11/2023. If Petitioner does not wish to pursue this petition, a written withdrawal of petition can be filed. Otherwise, petitioner needs to file other required documents two weeks prior to the hearing. This matter still appears premature for examiner's review as the following documents are not on file: Proof of publication; Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative; Notice of Petition to Administer Estate and Proposed Order for Probate. As to Petition for Probate itself, the following procedural issues/clarifications need to be made by filing a verified supplemental declaration: (1) Petition #3.f.(2) to allege whether the decedent's will being offered is a self-proving will or not. (2) Petition #5.a.(7)-(8) to be completed re: issue of a predeceased child. (3) Petition #8 to list and identify, as #8 instructs, next of kin of the decedent as Petition #6 affirms so. (4) Petition #8 to list relationship to decedent as the instruction specifies. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
